skill.name: migrate-to-shoehorn
skill.description: Migrate test files from `as` type assertions to @total-typescript/shoehorn. Use when user mentions shoehorn, wants to replace `as` in tests, or needs partial test data.
skill.summary: Migrate TypeScript test files from unsafe `as` assertions to type-safe shoehorn helpers like fromPartial() and fromAny().
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: All 37 static findings are false positives. The 'backtick execution' detections are Markdown code fences around TypeScript examples. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' flags are pattern false-positives from prose matching. The skill is benign documentation that guides migration of test code to use @total-typescript/shoehorn. No malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Shell command examples in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains bash and grep command examples (npm install, grep search). These are instructional, not executed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands shown are documentation examples for users to run manually, not code executed by the skill.

content.value_statement: TypeScript tests often use unsafe `as` assertions to fake large objects. This skill migrates them to type-safe shoehorn helpers, keeping autocomplete and type checking working.
actual_capabilities: Identifies test files with `as Type` and `as unknown as Type` assertions
Replaces `as Type` patterns with shoehorn's fromPartial() helper
Replaces `as unknown as Type` patterns with fromAny() helper
Provides migration guidance for large objects needing partial data
Helps select the right shoehorn function for each use case
limitations: Limited to test code only, not production code
Requires npm package @total-typescript/shoehorn to be installed
Does not automatically run type checks after migration
Focused on TypeScript test files only

use_cases.title: Migrate large mock objects in tests
use_cases.description: Replace verbose fakes of large TypeScript types with fromPartial() to pass only needed properties.

use_cases.title: Replace unsafe double-as assertions
use_cases.description: Convert `as unknown as Type` patterns to fromAny() for error testing scenarios.

use_cases.title: Standardize test data helpers
use_cases.description: Adopt shoehorn consistently across a codebase's test suite for better type safety.

prompt_templates.title: Basic migration request
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all test files using `as Type` assertions and migrate them to use fromPartial() from @total-typescript/shoehorn.

prompt_templates.title: Migrate double-as patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace all `as unknown as Type` patterns in my test files with fromAny() from @total-typescript/shoehorn.

prompt_templates.title: Migrate large object fakes
prompt_templates.prompt: I have test files that fake large Request objects with 20+ properties. Migrate them to use fromPartial() so I only specify the properties I need.

prompt_templates.title: Full audit and migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my test suite for all `as` assertions, install @total-typescript/shoehorn, and migrate every occurrence to the appropriate shoehorn helper.

output_examples.output: After: getUser(fromPartial({ body: { id: '123' } })); with the import added from @total-typescript/shoehorn.

output_examples.input: getUser({ body: { id: 123 } } as unknown as Request);
output_examples.output: After: getUser(fromAny({ body: { id: 123 } })); with the import added from @total-typescript/shoehorn.

best_practices: Use fromPartial() for large objects where only some properties are relevant to the test
Use fromAny() only when intentionally passing wrong-typed data for error testing
Keep shoehorn usage confined to test files, never production code
Run a type check after migration to confirm type safety is preserved
anti_patterns: Using shoehorn helpers in production source code
Migrating without verifying the type check still passes
Using fromAny() when fromPartial() would suffice, losing type safety benefits
Leaving some `as` assertions unmigrated, creating inconsistent test patterns

faq.question: What is shoehorn?
faq.answer: Shoehorn is a TypeScript library that lets you pass partial data in tests while keeping full type safety and autocomplete.

faq.question: Can shoehorn be used in production code?
faq.answer: No. Shoehorn is designed for test code only. Using it in production can mask real type errors.

faq.question: How do I install shoehorn?
faq.answer: Run `npm i @total-typescript/shoehorn` in your project to add the package.

faq.question: What is the difference between fromPartial and fromAny?
faq.answer: fromPartial() is for data that still type-checks correctly. fromAny() is for intentionally wrong data, such as error test cases.

faq.question: How do I find test files with `as` assertions?
faq.answer: Use the grep command shown in the skill workflow to find files matching ` as [A-Z]` in test files.

faq.question: Does this skill run code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and examples. You run the commands and edits yourself.
